reasoning: The control 3.4.6 focuses on employing the principle of least functionality, which aligns with several Kubernetes security practices. In Kubernetes, this can be enforced through RBAC by ensuring roles and permissions are limited to what is necessary (k8s_rbac_least_privilege_enforcement). NetworkPolicies can be used to restrict ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that only essential communication paths are open (k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_ingress). Pod Security Standards can enforce restrictions on pod capabilities, such as running as non-root, which aligns with limiting unnecessary functionality (k8s_pod_security_standard_restricted). Additionally, admission controllers can enforce security policies at the time of pod creation (k8s_admission_controller_pod_security_enabled).
title: 3_4_6: 3.4.6 Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities
